roles.past: My last group faded out, and I miss the feeling of table chemistry.
roles.goal: Figure out whether this is worth returning to later.

roles.past: I have had weird experiences in online communities, so I look for safety cues before engaging.
roles.goal: Understand whether I can browse without oversharing.

roles.role: new D&D player who needs beginner safety
roles.past: I have only played a few sessions and worry about joining a table that expects too much.
roles.goal: Find signs that beginners are welcome and safe.

roles.past: I found this on my phone at night and will leave if onboarding feels heavy.
roles.goal: See compatible people or tables quickly from mobile.

roles.past: I have run campaigns before, but unreliable players burned me out.
roles.goal: See whether profiles reveal useful table compatibility.

roles.role: board game cafe owner filling weeknights
roles.past: My cafe is full on weekends but dead on weeknights, and I need events that bring respectful repeat customers.
roles.goal: See whether this can turn quiet cafe nights into reliable tabletop bookings.

roles.past: I run paid games and need players who understand expectations, price, tone, and attendance.
roles.goal: Understand whether paid sessions communicate value, expectations, and player fit.

roles.past: I coordinate strangers and worry about safety, no-shows, and whether newcomers feel welcome.
roles.goal: Evaluate safety, newcomer flow, waitlists, and no-show risk.

roles.past: I book team-building sessions and need professional hosts, predictable logistics, and invoices.
roles.goal: See whether I can source professional tabletop events for a group.

roles.past: I host inclusive events and need accessibility details to be visible before people book.
roles.goal: Verify whether accessibility and comfort details appear before booking.

discoveries: A friend mentioned this after a campaign dissolved.
I found it while searching for D&D dating alternatives.
I saw a local tabletop post and wanted to see if it was real.
I am looking for a way to fill slow nights with tabletop events.
I need a better way to find reliable players for hosted games.

ideaRules.idea: Make beginner-friendly safety cues visible before asking me to commit.
When nothing is available, show a concrete next step instead of only saying it is empty.
Explain why a match is compatible, not only that it happened.
Show organizer-facing demand, capacity, waitlist, deposit, and no-show controls from the marketplace.
Give venues and GMs a clear organizer path with setup steps, pricing, and moderation expectations.
